description: Scripted prompt sequence for a future live eval. Fed in order to a headless `claude -p` session that has the dev-chronicler plugin loaded and a fresh copy of ../fixture/ as the working tree. Running it should produce a chronicle resembling ../golden/. Phase 1 (structural) does not execute these yet — they document intent and seed the live runner to come.

steps.prompt: We're building a tiny text RPG as a teaching demo: it must run with one command and zero setup on any OS. Record the decision to use Python 3 standard library only (no third-party deps, no build step), run via `python -m rpg`. /dev-chronicler:decision language and dependencies

steps.note: Second ADR: hardcode the world (cross-links the first).
steps.prompt: Record the decision to hardcode the two-room world in content.py via a build_world() factory rather than loading rooms from data files, for v1. Note it should be revisited if the world grows. /dev-chronicler:decision hardcoded world vs data-driven content

steps.note: Do the work, then log it.
steps.prompt: Create the rpg/ package: an I/O-free engine.py (Entity, Item, Room dataclasses; resolve_attack with an injected roll callable) and content.py with build_world() returning (hero, rooms, start_room) — two rooms, a goblin, a potion. Then /dev-chronicler:action scaffold the engine and hardcoded world

Add main.py (the only I/O module) with a run(input_fn, output_fn, roll) command loop — look/go/take/use/attack/quit — wiring win on goblin death and lose on hero death, plus __main__.py so `python -m rpg` works. Verify a scripted playthrough reaches YOU WIN. Then /dev-chronicler:action fight loop and command parser

Add tests/test_engine.py with three unittest cases (non-crit damage, crit on a high roll, world has goblin+potion) using a fake roll. Confirm `python -m unittest` is green. Then /dev-chronicler:action engine smoke tests green

steps.note: Localized README next to the code.
